pattern: Two passes over the grid: first tally the number of servers in each row and each column, then count every server whose row tally or column tally exceeds one.
hints: A server communicates when it shares its row or column with at least one other server. Think about precomputing a count per row and per column.
Scan the grid once to build row_count[r] and col_count[c], then scan again to test each server cell.
A server at (r,c) is communicating if row_count[r] > 1 OR col_count[c] > 1. Sum those up.

edge_inputs.description: 1x1 grid, single server — no partner possible
all servers in one row, all communicate
all servers in one column, all communicate
two servers in same column separated by many empty rows
fully packed grid, every server communicates
server that shares row with one partner but that partner only touches this server

stress.description: 250x250 grid with random server placement — checks O(m*n) path at max size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = size
    n = size
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
